TYPICAL PEDON.content:
Typical Pedon:
Rosegulch silty clay in urban, park. (Colors are for dry soil unless otherwise noted.)
^A--0 to 12 cm; brown (10YR 4/3) clay loam, dark brown (10YR 3/3) moist; moderate fine granular structure; slightly hard, friable, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; many very fine and common fine roots; noneffervescent, neutral (pH 7.2); clear smooth boundary.
^Bw-12 to 29 cm; light yellowish brown (2.5Y 6/4) clay loam, light olive brown (2.5Y 5/4) moist; weak medium subangular blocky parting to moderate medium granular structure; moderately hard, firm, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; common very fine and few fine roots; finely disseminated free carbonates throughout the matrix; 10 percent fine gravel; slightly effervescent; slightly alkaline (pH 7.8); gradual wavy boundary.
2Bkb1--29 to 66 cm; yellowish brown (10YR 5/6) silty clay, dark yellowish brown (10YR 4/6) moist; weak medium subangular blocky structure; slightly hard, friable, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; finely disseminated free carbonates and 8 percent medium, irregular carbonate masses  throughout the matrix; strongly effervescent; slightly alkaline (pH 7.8); gradual wavy boundary.
2Bkb2--66 to 106 cm; yellowish brown (10YR 5/4) silty clay, yellowish brown (10YR 5/6) moist; weak coarse subangular blocky structure; slightly hard, friable, moderately sticky, slightly plastic; finely disseminated free carbonates and 5 percent medium irregular carbonate masses throughout the matrix; slightly effervescent; slightly alkaline (pH 7.6); gradual wavy boundary.
^2Ck--106 to 200 cm; dark yellowish brown (10YR 4/4) silty clay, dark yellowish brown (10YR 3/4) moist; massive; moderately hard, firm, very sticky, very plastic; finely disseminated free carbonates and 1 percent fine threads of carbonate throughout the matrix; slightly effervescent; slightly alkaline (pH 7.6).
Type Location:
Denver County, CO
USGS Quadrangle: Englewood
Latitude: 39.6548611
Longitude: -104.8818139
Datum: WGS84
Coordinates sources: Hand-held GPS
Range in Characteristics:
Depth to free carbonates--10 to 200 centimeters
Thickness of human-transported material-less than 50 centimeters
Particle-size control section (weighted average):
Clay content--40 to 50 percent
Sand content--10 to 20 percent
Total rock fragments--0 to 5 percent
^A horizon:
Hue-- 10YR
Value--4 to 6 dry; 1 or 3 moist
Chroma--3 to 5 dry; 3 or 4 moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Silty clay loam, clay loam
Clay content--28 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 15 percent gravel
Calcium carbonate equivalent--0 to 5 percent
Reaction--pH 7.2 to 8.0
Thickness--5 to 15 centimeters
^Bwb horizon(s):
Hue-- 10YR
Value--4 to 6 dry; 3 to 5 moist
Chroma--3 to 5 moist or dry
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Silty clay loam, clay loam
Clay content--28 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 20 percent gravel
Calcium carbonate equivalent--2 to 10 percent
Reaction--pH 7.6 to 8.2
Thickness--15 to 25 centimeters
2Bkb horizon(s) (where present):
Hue-2.5Y or 10YR
Value-4 to 6 dry; 4 or 5 moist
Chroma--3 to 5 dry; 4 or 5 moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Silty clay loam, silty clay, clay
Clay content--40 to 50 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 5 percent gravel
Calcium carbonate equivalent--5 to 14 percent
Reaction--pH 7.6 to 8.4
Thickness--25 to 60 centimeters (combined)
2Ck horizon(s):
Hue--2.5Y or 10YR
Value--4 to 6 dry; 4 or 5 moist
Chroma-4 to 6 dry; 4 or 5 moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Silty clay loam, silty clay, clay
Clay content--38 to 50 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 3 percent gravels
Calcium carbonate equivalent--5 to 14 percent
Reaction--pH 7.6 to 8.4
Thickness--25 to 65 centimeters (combined)
Competing Series: These are the Denby, Echeta, Gojiya, Kanorado, and Pierre series.  None of these series have an ^A horizon.
Geographic Settings:
Parent material-- Thin human-transported material over clayey Pleistocene-aged loess derived from glaciofluvial sources
Landform-- Human-transported fill on loess-mantled plains
Slope ranges--0 to 10 percent
Mean annual air temperature-10.0 to 12.0 degrees C (50.0 to 53.6 degrees F)
Mean annual precipitation range--356 to 432 millimeters (14.0 to 17.0 inches)
Frost-free period--135 to 165 days
Elevation--1,625 to 1,725 meters (~5,330 to 5,660 feet)
Climate--continental, temperate, semi-arid; very cold and snowy winters; cool, moist springs, hot summers, with North American monsoon precipitation from July through August.
Geographically Associated Soils:
Racas soils: Found in similar landscape positions, but Racas soils are spolic and lack cambic horizons
Drainage and Saturated Hydraulic Conductivity:
Drainage class: Well and moderately well drained
Saturated hydraulic conductivity: Low (0.01 to 0.5 mm/sec)
Flooding: Rosegulch soils are not subject to flooding
Ponding: Rosegulch soils are not subject to ponding
Use and Vegetation:
Most areas are primarily used for urban development. The potential native vegetation is fourwing saltbush, winterfat, western wheatgrass, and blue grama.
Distribution and Extent:
Loess-mantled plains, primarily on the east bank of the South Platte River near Denver, CO; small extent in north-central Colorado; MLRA 67B.
Soil Survey Region (SSR) Responsible:
Bozeman, Montana
Series Proposed:
Denver County, CO 2025. Rosegulch series is a coined name made from combining the names of neighborhoods near the type location.
Remarks:
Typical Pedon Taxonomic Features:
Ochric epipedon--0 to 12 centimeters (^A horizon)
Cambic horizon-12 to 29 centimeters (^Bw horizon
Free carbonates--12 to 200 centimeters (^Bw, 2Bkb1, 2Bkb2, 2Ck)
Human-transported materials--0 to 29 centimeters (^A, ^Bw horizons)
Lithologic discontinuity-29 centimeters
Particle-size control section--25 to 100 centimeters
Taxonomic version: Keys to Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition, (2022)
